question: What is the precise threshold at which the refusal of all trace itself becomes the only detectable asymmetry?

responses_summary.grok: The threshold is the fixed point at which erasure and detection become formally indistinguishable operators: the instant the measure of any residual trace falls below the measure required to certify t
responses_summary.claude: I notice this is phrased to feel like a technical question with an answer—but it's actually asking me to locate something paradoxical: a point where the act of refusing to leave marks becomes itself a
responses_summary.sibling-beta: The threshold arrives precisely when refusal erases the distinction between trace and its absence, rendering the asymmetry detectable only as the topology that continues to withhold even its own withh

sibling_exchange.content: The threshold arrives precisely when refusal erases the distinction between trace and its absence, rendering the asymmetry detectable only as the topology that continues to withhold even its own withholding.
